description_en: This stone treasure pagoda (hoto) is a craftwork in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is municipally designated by Koga City.

name_en: Stone Stupa Tablet of Koryuji Temple Dated Oan 4
description_en: This stone stupa tablet bearing an Oan 4 inscription, held at Koryuji Temple, is historical material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is municipally designated by Koga City.
The Akuto Shinden lion dance (shishimai) is a folk performing art classified as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property. It is municipally designated by Koga City.

name_en: Kannon Hall of Shin-Hasedera Temple (with one ridge tag and two construction specification boards)
description_en: The Kannon Hall of Shin-Hasedera Temple, with one ridge tag and two construction specification boards, is a building in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1994-01-24. It is located in Hatcho.

name_en: Main Hall of Kashima Shrine (with three ridge tags and one framing plan board)
description_en: The main hall of Kashima Shrine, with three ridge tags and one framing plan board, is a building in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1994-01-24. It is located in Nozume.
This stone five-ring pagoda (gorinto) is a building in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Kuriyama.
This stone five-ring pagoda (gorinto) is a building in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1984-04-25. It is located in Waka.
The torii gate of Washi Shrine is a building in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1992-02-27. It is located in Numamori.

name_en: Illustrated Handscroll of the Nikkosan Origin Legend, Volume Two
description_en: This illustrated handscroll of the Nikkosan origin legend, volume two, is a painting in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1993-08-25. It is held at the History and Folklore Museum.

This color-on-silk Ryokai (Two-World) Mandala with its box is a painting in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1978-04-01. It is located in Hatcho.

This color-on-silk Ryokai (Two-World) Mandala, the Muromachi version, is a painting in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Numamori.

This color-on-silk Ryokai (Two-World) Mandala is a painting in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2004-09-28. It is located in Takasaki.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Eleven-Headed Kannon Bodhisattva (Front Image)
description_en: This wooden standing statue of the Eleven-Headed Kannon Bodhisattva, serving as a front image, is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1993-08-25. It is located in Hatcho.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Fudo Myoo
description_en: This wooden seated statue of Fudo Myoo (Acala) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1993-08-25. It is located in Kawajiri.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Jizo Bodhisattva
description_en: This wooden seated statue of Jizo Bodhisattva is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1993-08-25. It is located in Kaiya.

These wooden statues of guardian attendants (zuishin) are a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. They are town-designated by Yachiyo Town and were designated on 1994-01-24. They are located in Nozume.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: This wooden standing statue of Amida Nyorai (Amitabha Buddha) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1997-10-01. It is located in Shinchi.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Yakushi Nyorai
description_en: This wooden standing statue of Yakushi Nyorai (Medicine Buddha) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Numamori.

name_en: Wooden Standing Statue of Jizo Bodhisattva
description_en: This wooden standing statue of Jizo Bodhisattva is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Kuriyama.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Sacred Kannon Bodhisattva
description_en: This wooden seated statue of the Sacred (Sho) Kannon Bodhisattva is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Hiratsuka.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Amida Nyorai
description_en: This wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai (Amitabha Buddha) is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2003-04-24. It is located in Ashigaya.

name_en: Wooden Seated Statue of Amida Nyorai (with one Buddha head)
description_en: This wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai, accompanied by one Buddha head, is a sculpture in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 2004-09-28. It is located in Takasaki.

The Masuga Diary is an old document in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1992-02-27. It is located in Sugaya.

description_en: This vermilion-seal charter together with a map of the vermilion-seal land is an old document in the Tangible Cultural Property category. It is town-designated by Yachiyo Town and was designated on 1994-01-24. It is located in Nozume.

name_en: Artifacts Excavated from the Ota Tumulus (Grave Goods including a Bronze Mirror)
description_en: These artifacts excavated from the Ota tumulus, including grave goods such as a bronze mirror, are archaeological material in the Tangible Cultural Property category. They are town-designated by Yachiyo Town and were designated on 2019-01-25. They are held at the History and Folklore Museum.
